skill.name: worldview-synthesis
skill.description: Guides structured exploration of personal values, beliefs, tensions, and life philosophy, then turns the results into mission statements, theses, and longer worldview narratives.
skill.summary: Structure personal beliefs into a clear worldview narrative.
skill.author: 2389-research

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported weak cryptography, shell backtick execution, and system reconnaissance patterns, but review found these are false positives from Markdown prose, tables, and fenced examples. No executable scripts, network calls, credential access, or prompt injection attempts were found in the reviewed files. The remaining risk is low because the skill asks users to disclose sensitive beliefs, values, politics, spirituality, and life history.

low_findings.title: Sensitive Personal Reflection Data
low_findings.description: The skill intentionally asks about mortality, spirituality, relationships, vices, politics, ethics, and personal worldview. This is not malicious, but users should avoid sharing secrets or identifying details unless they intend to store them in generated notes.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed text directly instructs the assistant to ask about sensitive personal domains. There is no evidence of exfiltration or hidden execution, so confidence is high for a low privacy-content concern only.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Static Finding: Markdown Backticks
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged Ruby or shell backtick execution in SKILL.md. The cited locations are Markdown fences and examples for directory trees, YAML-like data, question rounds, and sample output, not executable shell commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The relevant lines are visibly Markdown fenced content and contain no command runner, interpreter call, or user-controlled execution path. This is a clear false positive.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Static Finding: Weak Crypto and Reconnaissance Terms
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged weak cryptographic algorithm and system reconnaissance patterns in prose. Review found no cryptographic API usage, hashing implementation, host inspection, process listing, or system probing behavior in the skill files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content is natural-language guidance and schema prose. No evidence found for cryptographic operations or system reconnaissance behavior.

content.value_statement: Many people hold strong beliefs but struggle to explain how those beliefs fit together. This skill uses structured questions and synthesis steps to turn values, tensions, and influences into clear narratives.
actual_capabilities: Guides users through structured worldview interrogation rounds.
Maps beliefs into ideas, sources, tensions, and narrative outputs.
Identifies contradictions without forcing premature resolution.
Provides reusable question sets across personal, ethical, social, and future-facing domains.
Generates mission statements, theses, synopses, and longer worldview narratives.
Defines simple schemas for organizing belief nodes and unresolved tensions.
limitations: It depends on honest user input and cannot infer private beliefs reliably.
It is not therapy, legal advice, political advice, or spiritual counseling.
It may surface sensitive memories or values that require careful handling.
It does not verify factual claims made by the user or their sources.

use_cases.title: Define a Leadership Philosophy
use_cases.description: Turn scattered beliefs about work, trust, conflict, and ethics into a concise leadership statement.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Personal Manifesto
use_cases.description: Explore values, influences, and tensions before writing a public or private worldview essay.

use_cases.title: Clarify Life Priorities
use_cases.description: Use guided questions to understand beliefs about mortality, family, money, society, and the future.

prompt_templates.title: Start My Worldview Map
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me start a worldview map. Ask me the first round of questions about my core values and influences.

prompt_templates.title: Find My Tensions
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my answers and identify the strongest tensions in my beliefs. Do not resolve them too quickly.

prompt_templates.title: Write a Mission Statement
prompt_templates.prompt: Use my belief notes to write a mission statement with one clear thesis and five guiding principles.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Full Worldview Narrative
prompt_templates.prompt: Synthesize my ideas, sources, tensions, and non-negotiables into a structured worldview essay with a clear voice.

output_examples.input: I value independence, but I also believe people survive through strong communities.
output_examples.output: A useful tension is autonomy versus mutual obligation. The narrative can show independence as a capacity that becomes stronger when paired with trusted community.

output_examples.input: I want a short leadership philosophy for my team.
output_examples.output: Lead with clarity, protect trust, and make hard decisions early. Treat conflict as information, not as a personal attack.

output_examples.input: I have notes about work, family, religion, and politics, but they feel disconnected.
output_examples.output: The skill can group those notes into domains, name recurring principles, and draft a thesis that connects private values with public choices.

best_practices: Answer questions with concrete experiences, not only abstract ideals.
Keep unresolved tensions visible until a real synthesis emerges.
Review generated narratives for voice, privacy, and accuracy before sharing.
anti_patterns: Do not treat the first draft as a final identity statement.
Do not force every contradiction into a neat resolution.
Do not include secrets or identifying details in prompts unless storage is intended.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates structured belief notes, named tensions, mission statements, short theses, synopses, and longer worldview narratives.

faq.question: Is this only for philosophy experts?
faq.answer: No. It uses plain questions about everyday choices, relationships, work, ethics, and the future.

faq.question: Can it help with company culture?
faq.answer: Yes. It can turn founder or team beliefs into principles, tensions, and leadership language.

faq.question: Does it decide what I should believe?
faq.answer: No. It helps surface, organize, and express beliefs that come from the user.

faq.question: Is it safe for sensitive topics?
faq.answer: Use care. The questions can involve politics, spirituality, family, death, money, and personal history.

faq.question: Can I use it in Claude, Codex, or Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
